title: Historical Quality Knowledge
normalized_content:
Phase 0.4 Historical Quality Knowledge for Aetheris
BioPharma
This document defines the historical quality-event layer for Aetheris BioPharma Private
Limited using the permanent foundation established in Phases 0.1, 0.2, and 0.3. It creates
internally consistent historical investigations, CAPAs, deviations, complaints, and audit
reports linked to the same employees, sites, products, suppliers, equipment, and document
identifiers already established in the Aetheris enterprise model.
Scope
The records in this phase are intended to simulate the historical knowledge that a mature
quality management platform would ingest to support investigations, evidence retrieval, audit
preparation, trend analysis, and AI-assisted organizational memory. All identifiers,
relationships, ownership lines, and operational references remain consistent with the Aetheris
BioPharma foundation.
Historical record architecture
This phase creates and links the following record families:
● Historical deviations
● Historical investigations
● Historical CAPAs
● Historical complaints
● Historical audit reports
● Cross-record traceability between sources, investigations, CAPAs, products, equipment,
employees, and suppliers

Deviation relationship notes
● PASH-DEV-2025-0142 links to PASH-BMR-FPP1001-2503-001 (historical batch not
previously listed in Phase 0.3 but valid under the same numbering rules).
● VISK-DEV-2025-0094 links to environmental monitoring data under the VISK-EM record
family and to ongoing sterile trend review.
● CORP-DEV-2025-0031 links to CORP-VAL-CSV-2025-001 because the event concerns
workflow and audit-trail controls in the enterprise QMS platform.

3. Historical investigations
Investigation numbering follows the established event conventions and remains event-type
specific. For simplicity in the Aetheris evidence model, each deviation or complaint
investigation is tracked under its source identifier, with formal investigation reports
referenced by a report document number.

Investigation evidence fields
Each historical investigation package is understood to include:
● Source event summary
● Scope and impact assessment
● Product or batch impact review
● Equipment and utility review where applicable
● Employee interview summary
● Root cause analysis method used
● Corrective and preventive action recommendation
● QA approval and closure rationale

CAPA detail summaries
PASH-CAPA-2025-0037

● Source: PASH-CMP-2025-0048
● Issue: Tablet breakage in blister cavity linked to localized hardness variation.
● Actions:
○ Tightened compression-force operating range on EQ-OSD-01-002.
○ Added hourly hardness trend escalation rule in ML-OSD-01.
○ Revised blister packing challenge check before batch start.
● Effectiveness result: No recurrence in three subsequent Aethcard batches.
VISK-CAPA-2025-0028
● Source: VISK-CMP-2025-0026
● Issue: Visible cosmetic particulate complaint in Injectra vial.
● Actions:
○ Enhanced washer-cycle verification for EQ-ST-01-001.
○ Added particulate-focused line clearance checkpoint for EQ-ST-01-002.
○ Retrained sterile operators and inspectors.
● Effectiveness result: Enhanced visual inspection trend remained within internal limit for
60 days.
CORP-CAPA-2025-0008
● Source: CORP-DEV-2025-0031
● Issue: Incomplete contemporaneous rationale in enterprise QMS workflow.
● Actions:
○ Workflow field changed to mandatory for closure-rationale audit trail.
○ QA review checklist updated.
○ IT and QA administrators retrained on workflow governance.
● Effectiveness result: Quarterly audit-trail review found no missing rationale entries.
VISK-CAPA-2026-0026
● Source: VISK-CMP-2026-0021
● Issue: Lyophilized cake reconstitution delay complaint for FPP-2003.
● Actions:
○ Reviewed lyophilization cycle parameter bands.
○ Added trend threshold for shelf-temperature drift.
○ Updated batch review step for reconstitution-supporting in-process evidence.
● Effectiveness result: Three subsequent batches met reconstitution trend expectations.

8. Historical knowledge rules for future phases

The historical records created in this phase establish precedent for how future events should
be written and linked.
● Every complaint, deviation, or audit finding may trigger a formal investigation.
● Every investigation must indicate whether CAPA was required.
● Every CAPA must identify effectiveness verification method and closure basis.
● Audit findings should reference the same SOPs, equipment, products, and departments
defined in the Aetheris foundation.
● No future record should introduce a conflicting employee, site, or product identifier
without an explicit organizational change record.
9. Completion state:
Establishes the historical quality-knowledge layer for Aetheris BioPharma.
Created in this phase:
● Historical deviations
● Historical complaint records
● Historical investigation register and report identifiers
● Historical CAPA register and action summaries
● Historical audit report register and findings summaries
● Cross-record traceability between events and operational evidence
